headline: General item capture failed when collection was missing
hypothesis: General's item_create tool enforced a pre-existing collection even though MCP capture flows can select a new collection name directly.

blurb_segments.text: The recorded failure was a ValueError from general.item_create for a missing collection named episodes.

blurb_segments: The tool now creates the target collection as part of item insertion, so first-write capture no longer fails.

c1.note: The failing MCP call reached General item creation and stopped because the target collection did not exist.

evidence_lines.msg: MCP tool call failed for general.item_create: ValueError: Collection 'episodes' not found.

counter_evidence.hypothesis: The database schema was missing the collections table.
counter_evidence.reason: The error came from the explicit collection lookup returning no row, not from an undefined table or migration failure.

counter_evidence.hypothesis: The model selected an invalid General collection name.
counter_evidence.reason: General collections are intentionally user-defined freeform names; episodes is a valid name even if it did not exist yet.

why_this_fix: Creating or resolving the collection inside item_create removes the brittle ordering requirement while preserving the existing collection uniqueness constraint. Explicit collection_create remains available for callers that want to attach descriptions.

diff_snapshot.text: WITH target_collection AS (INSERT INTO collections (name) VALUES ($1) ON CONFLICT (name) DO UPDATE ...)
INSERT INTO collection_items (collection_id, data, tags) SELECT id, $2, $3 FROM target_collection
